skill.name: chapter-analyzer
skill.description: Validates and analyzes Docusaurus MDX chapters for structure, pedagogical quality, and component usage.
skill.summary: Validates and analyzes Docusaurus MDX chapters for structure, pedagogical quality, and component usa...
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found the cited locations are Markdown inline code, YAML frontmatter examples, and MDX component examples in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Syntax Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline backticks and fenced documentation examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are Markdown formatting for target paths, file extensions, heading examples, and MDX component names, not executable code. The duplicate report on line 10 is also a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited lines are inside SKILL.md documentation and contain Markdown or MDX examples. There is no shell command, script file, or command execution directive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: MDX Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on lines that contain the skill description and a YAML description field example. The text refers to MDX chapter documentation and does not invoke or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose and YAML sample metadata only. No hashing, encryption, crypto library, or algorithm usage appears in the file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged lines describing chapter metadata and writing clarity as system reconnaissance. These lines define expected frontmatter and editing guidance, not host, user, process, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is chapter validation guidance and sample frontmatter. No commands, APIs, or instructions for system enumeration are present.

content.value_statement: Course authors need consistent MDX chapters that teach clearly. This skill checks structure, components, and learning flow for Docusaurus content.
actual_capabilities: Reviews chapters under textbook/docs for Docusaurus MDX conventions.
Checks required YAML frontmatter fields for id, title, sidebar label, and description.
Evaluates heading hierarchy so body content starts below the page title.
Looks for expected teaching flow, including objectives, core content, interactions, and summary.
Checks use of Quiz, Simulation, Tabs, and TabItem components in MDX chapters.
limitations: It provides review guidance and does not include an automated parser or test runner.
It assumes the project uses Docusaurus and the textbook/docs path.
It cannot verify runtime behavior of custom MDX components by itself.
It does not replace subject matter review for technical accuracy.

use_cases.title: Review a New Lesson
use_cases.description: Check a draft chapter for required metadata, heading order, and learner-friendly structure before review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Existing Docs
use_cases.description: Find chapters that drift from Docusaurus frontmatter and component conventions across a textbook.

use_cases.title: Prepare Interactive Course Content
use_cases.description: Confirm that major sections include quizzes, simulations, or tabbed examples where they support learning.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Docusaurus MDX chapter using the chapter-analyzer skill. Report missing frontmatter, heading issues, and teaching flow gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Component Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of interactive MDX elements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the chapter for Quiz, Simulation, Tabs, and TabItem usage. Suggest where interactive elements would improve major sections.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Textbook Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze chapters in textbook/docs for consistent frontmatter, heading hierarchy, summaries, and learning objectives. Group issues by chapter.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Editorial Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a publication readiness review for the Docusaurus chapters. Prioritize structural blockers, pedagogy gaps, and component consistency.

output_examples.input: A chapter starts with a body H1 and has no learning objectives.
output_examples.output: The review flags the body H1 as a hierarchy issue and recommends adding clear learning objectives near the introduction.

output_examples.input: A chapter has valid frontmatter but no quiz or simulation after long sections.
output_examples.output: The review notes that metadata is complete and suggests adding an interactive check after major conceptual sections.

output_examples.input: A chapter uses Python and C++ examples in separate headings.
output_examples.output: The review suggests using tabbed examples so readers can compare languages without duplicated section structure.

best_practices: Run the review before merging chapters into a published Docusaurus site.
Pair structural feedback with a separate technical accuracy review.
Keep interactive elements tied to specific learning objectives.
anti_patterns: Do not treat this skill as a full MDX build validator.
Do not add quizzes or simulations where they do not support comprehension.
Do not ignore missing frontmatter because a local preview still renders.

faq.question: What type of content does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews Docusaurus MDX or Markdown chapters, especially educational content in textbook/docs.

faq.question: Can it fix chapters automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide edits and suggest changes, but the skill itself defines review rules rather than an automated fixer.

faq.question: Does it validate MDX syntax at build time?
faq.answer: No. Use the Docusaurus build process to confirm syntax and component runtime behavior.

faq.question: What frontmatter fields does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects id, title, sidebar_label, and description in chapter frontmatter.

faq.question: Does it require interactive components?
faq.answer: It expects at least one quiz or simulation per major section when that supports the lesson.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
